title: John Swinney may consider banning US military from Prestwick Airport

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: John Swinney may consider banning US military from Prestwick Airport

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: The First Minister said he would 'consider' banning the US military from using Prestwick Airport (Image: NQ) This article is brought to you by our exclusive subscriber partnership with our sister title USA Today, and has been written by our American colleagues. It does not necessarily reflect the view of The Herald.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: JOHN Swinney has said he would "consider" banning US military aircraft from using Prestwick Airport if it was confirmed they were involved in strikes on Iran. The First Minister has previously suggested the US and Israel broke international law in launching strikes on Iran, as he also warned Keir Starmer's decision to let the US use UK bases in its operations is making the situation more dangerous.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – John Swinney would 'consider' banning US military from Prestwick Airport
